mnemonics: A Stationary process is like a Steady State: Its Statistical properties (Mean, Variance, Covariance) Stay the same over time.
keyPoints: Weak Stationarity requires a constant mean and a covariance that depends only on the lag, not on time.
Strong Stationarity requires the entire probability distribution to be time-invariant.
Most models (like AR, MA, ARMA) are built on the assumption of stationarity.
commonMistakes: Assuming a trendless plot implies stationarity (it must also have constant variance).
Using non-stationary data in models that require stationarity, leading to spurious results.
